title: pimelea orthia subsp. orthia
intro: Pimelea orthia subsp. orthia, also known as the 'Rabbit's Brush' or 'Native Pepper Tree', is a plant species native to Australia. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Pimelea orthia subsp. orthia include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused oil, and in some cases, the leaves and flowers are dried and used in smudging ceremonies for their cleansing and protective properties.
